dict.string: The human-readable description of this payload. This description appears on the Detail screen.
Optional. A human-readable description of this payload. This description is shown on the Detail screen.
The human-readable name for the profile payload. The name appears on the Detail screen and doesn't need to be unique.
A human-readable name for the profile payload. This name is displayed on the Detail screen. It does not have to be unique.

The reverse-DNS-style identifier for the payload. This identifier is usually the same as the TopLevel value, with an additional appended component. This string must be unique within the profile. During a profile replacement, the system updates payloads with the same 'PayloadIdentifier' and 'PayloadUUID' in the old and new profiles.
A reverse-DNS-style identifier for the specific payload. It is usually the same identifier as the root-level PayloadIdentifier value with an additional component appended.
The payload type, which each payload domain's reference page specifies.

The globally unique identifier for the payload. The actual content is unimportant, but must be globally unique. In macOS, use 'uuidgen' to generate UUIDs. During a profile replacement, the system updates payloads with the same 'PayloadIdentifier' and 'PayloadUUID' in the old and new profiles.
A globally unique identifier for the payload. The actual content is unimportant, but it must be globally unique. In macOS, you can use uuidgen to generate reasonable UUIDs.
The version of this specific payload.

The version number of the individual payload. A profile can consist of payloads with different version numbers. For example, changes to the VPN software in iOS might introduce a new payload version to support additional features, but Mail payload versions would not necessarily change in the same release.
The human-readable string containing the name of the organization that provides the profile. This value doesn't need to match the organization payload value in the enclosing dictionary.

Optional. A human-readable string containing the name of the organization that provided the profile. The payload organization for a payload need not match the payload organization in the enclosing profile.
The client mode that Santa should operate in.
1 = MONITOR, 2 = LOCKDOWN, 3 = STANDALONE, defaults to MONITOR
Deny execution in LOCKDOWN mode if there is an error reading or processing an executable file.
If true and the ClientMode is LOCKDOWN: execution will be denied when there is an error reading or processing an executable file.
Path regexes (ICU format) to monitor for file changes.
The regex of paths to log file changes. Regexes are specified in ICU format.
Path prefixes to exclude from file change logging.
Array of path prefix strings. When an event is logged, if the target path (e.g. the file being written/removed/etc) matches a prefix it will not be logged.
Types of telemetry events that should be logged.
Array of strings for events that should be logged.
Path regexes (ICU format) of binaries to allow. Lower precedent than binary, certificate, and Team ID scopes.
A regex to allow if the binary, certificate, or Team ID scopes did not allow/block execution. Regexes are specified in ICU format.
Path regexes (ICU format) of binaries to block. Lower precedent than binary, certificate, and Team ID scopes.
A regex to block if the binary, certificate, or Team ID scopes did not allow/block an execution. Regexes are specified in ICU format.
Block binaries with a bad signing chain, unless explicitly allowed.
Enable bad signature protection, defaults to NO. If this flag is set to YES, binaries with a bad signing chain will be blocked even in MONITOR mode, unless the binary is allowed by an explicit rule.
Block 32-bit binaries missing the __PAGEZERO segment, unless explicitly allowed.

Enable __PAGEZERO protection, defaults to YES. If this flag is set to YES, 32-bit binaries that are missing the __PAGEZERO segment will be blocked even in MONITOR mode, unless the binary is allowed by an explicit rule.
Allow transitive rules created by compiler-type rules.
If true, Santa will respect compiler rule types and create transitive allow rules for the executables they produce. If false, ALLOWLIST_COMPILER rules will be treated as ALLOWLIST rules.
If true, Santa will not post any GUI notifications. This can be a very confusing experience for users, use with caution.
If true, Santa will not post any TTY notifications. This can be a very confusing experience for users, use with caution.
Show the Santa menu bar item.
If true, Santa will show an item in the macOS menu bar to allow syncing and management of temporary monitor mode by default. Users are able to show/hide the menu item from the About window.
Custom text to display at launch.
The text to display when the user opens Santa.app. If unset, the default text will be displayed.
The URL to open when the user clicks "More Info..." when opening Santa.app. If unset, the button will not be displayed.

The URL to open when the user gets a block notification. If unset, the button will not be displayed. Can include the following variables for substitution: %file_identifier% - SHA-256 of the file that was blocked %bundle_or_file_identifier% - SHA-256 of the file that was blocked or the bundle containing it, if available %file_sha% - Deprecated, acts like bundle_or_file_identifier %machine_id% - ID of the machine %username% - The executing user %serial% - System's serial number %uuid% - System's UUID %hostname% - System's full hostname

When the user gets a block notification, a button can be displayed which will take them to a web page with more information about that event. This property contains a kind of format string to be turned into the URL to send them to. The following sequences will be replaced in the final URL: %file_identifier% - SHA-256 of the file that was blocked %bundle_or_file_identifier% - SHA-256 of the file that was blocked or the bundle containing it, if available %file_sha% - Deprecated, acts like bundle_or_file_identifier %machine_id% - ID of the machine %username% - The executing user %serial% - System's serial number %uuid% - System's UUID %hostname% - System's full hostname For example: https://sync-server-hostname/%machine_id%/%file_sha%
The text of the button for Event Detail URL.
Related to the EventDetailURL property, this string represents the text to show on the button.

The URL to open when the user gets a file access block notification. If unset, the button will not be displayed. Can include the following variables for substitution: %rule_version% - Version of the rule that was violated %rule_name% - Name of the rule that was violated %accessed_path% - The path accessed by the binary %file_identifier% - SHA-256 of the binary that performed the access %username% - The executing user %team_id% - The Team ID that signed the binary, if any %signing_id% - The Signing ID of the binary, if any %cdhash% - The binary's CDHash, if any %machine_id% - ID of the machine %hostname% - System's full hostname %uuid% - System's UUID %serial% - System's serial number

When the user gets a file access block notification, a button can be displayed which will take them to a web page with more information about that event. This URL will be used for all file access rules unless overridden by a rule-specific option. This property supports several placeholders in the string that will be replaced before the URL is constructed. The following sequences will be replaced in the final URL: %rule_version% - Version of the rule that was violated %rule_name% - Name of the rule that was violated %accessed_path% - The path accessed by the binary %file_identifier% - SHA-256 of the binary that performed the access %username% - The executing user %team_id% - The Team ID that signed the binary, if any %signing_id% - The Signing ID of the binary, if any %cdhash% - The binary's CDHash, if any %machine_id% - ID of the machine %hostname% - System's full hostname %uuid% - System's UUID %serial% - System's serial number
The text of the button for File Access Event Detail URL.
Related to the FileAccessEventDetailURL property, this string represents the text to show on the button.
Custom message when an unknown binary is blocked.
In Lockdown mode this is the message shown to the user when an unknown binary is blocked. If this message is not configured a reasonable default is provided.
This application has been blocked from executing.
The text to display on the button that dismisses the binary block dialog.
The text to display on the button that dismisses the binary block dialog. The default text is "Dismiss".
Custom message when a binary is blocked by a rule.
This is the message shown to the user when a binary is blocked because of a rule if that rule doesn't provide a custom message. If this is not configured a reasonable default is provided.
Custom message when entering MONITOR mode.
The notification text to display when the client goes into Monitor mode. Defaults to "Switching into Monitor mode".
Custom message when entering LOCKDOWN mode.
The notification text to display when the client goes into Lockdown mode. Defaults to "Switching into Lockdown mode".
Custom message when a Removable Media (e.g. USB device) is prevented from being mounted.
Message to display when a Removable Media (e.g. USB device) is prevented from being mounted.
Custom message when a Removable Media (e.g. USB device) is allowed to be mounted with a subset of the requested flags as defined by RemountUSBMode.
Message to display when a Removable Media (e.g. USB device) is allowed to be mounted with a subset of the requested flags as defined by RemountUSBMode.
Custom message when a file access is blocked by a rule.

This is the message shown to the user when access to a file is blocked because of a rule defined by FileAccessPolicy if that rule doesn't provide a custom message. If this is not configured a reasonable default is provided.
Access to a file has been denied
If false, the user will not be presented with an option to silence notifications.
Company name displayed in Santa UI. For GUI windows, this setting is ignored if BrandingCompanyLogo is set.
The company name to display on Santa GUIs as well as in messages written to the TTY. For GUI windows, this setting is ignored if BrandingCompanyLogo is set.

A URL referencing a logo image to display on Santa UIs. The image will be scaled down appropriately to fit within image bounds (currently 84x28 pixels). If set, this overrides BrandingCompanyName. Supported URL schemes: file:// and data:. HTTP/HTTPS URLs are not supported.

A URL referencing a logo image to display on Santa UIs. The image will be scaled down appropriately to fit within image bounds (currently 84x28 pixels). If set, this overrides BrandingCompanyName. Supported URL schemes: file:// and data:. HTTP/HTTPS URLs are not supported.

A URL referencing a logo image to display on Santa UIs. When the UI is displayed in dark mode and this is set, it overrides both BrandingCompanyLogo and BrandingCompanyName. Supported URL schemes: file:// and data:. HTTP/HTTPS URLs are not supported.

A URL referencing a logo image to display on Santa UIs. When the UI is displayed in dark mode and this is set, it overrides both BrandingCompanyLogo and BrandingCompanyName. Supported URL schemes: file:// and data:. HTTP/HTTPS URLs are not supported.
Enable themed fonts and images on April 1, May 4 and October 31.
If true, the Santa UI will use special images/fonts on certain holidays.
The base URL of the sync server.
Enable binary proto transfer for sync instead of JSON.
If true, sync will happen using binary protos instead of JSON.
Upload events to the sync server even if a clean sync is requested.
If true, events will be uploaded to the sync server even if a clean sync is requested. Defaults to false.
Location of a PKCS#12 certificate to be used for sync authentication.
If set, this contains the location of a PKCS#12 certificate to be used for sync authentication.
Contains the password for the PKCS#12 certificate.
The Common Name of a certificate in the System keychain to be used for sync authentication.
If set, this is the Common Name of a certificate in the System keychain to be used for sync authentication. The corresponding private key must also be in the keychain.
The corresponding private key must also be in the keychain.
The Issuer Name of a certificate in the System keychain to be used for sync authentication.
If set, this is the Issuer Name of a certificate in the System keychain to be used for sync authentication. The corresponding private key must also be in the keychain.
The corresponding private key must also be in the keychain.
X.509 certificate (.pem) for inclusion on device. A valid PEM containing one or more certificates to be used for certificate pinning.

If set, this is valid PEM containing one or more certificates to be used for certificate pinning. To comply with ATS - https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/documentation/General/Reference/InfoPlistKeyReference/Articles/CocoaKeys.html#//apple_ref/doc/uid/TP40009251-SW57 - the certificate chain must also be trusted in the keychain.

To comply with ATS (https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/documentation/General/Reference/InfoPlistKeyReference/Articles/CocoaKeys.html#//apple_ref/doc/uid/TP40009251-SW57) the certificate chain must also be trusted in the keychain.
Path to an on-disk X.509 certificate (.pem). A valid PEM containing one or more certificates to be used for certificate pinning.
The same as the above but is a path to a file on disk containing the PEM data.

To comply with ATS (https://developer.apple.com/library/archive/documentation/General/Reference/InfoPlistKeyReference/Articles/CocoaKeys.html#//apple_ref/doc/uid/TP40009251-SW57) the certificate chain must also be trusted in the keychain.
Groups the machine owner is a member of.
Groups the machine owner is a member of.
The path to a plist that contains the MachineOwnerKey / value pair.
The path to a plist that contains the MachineOwnerKey / value pair.
The key to use on Machine Owner Plist.
The key to use on MachineOwnerPlist.
The key to use on MachineOwnerPlist to access defined groups.
The key to use on MachineOwnerPlist to access defined groups.
The path to a plist that contains the MachineOwnerKey / value pair.
The path to a plist that contains the MachineOwnerKey / value pair.
The key to use on Machine ID Plist.
The key to use on MachineIDPlist.
Upload all execution events to the sync server, including those explicitly allowed.
If true, the client will upload all execution events to the sync server, including those that were explicitly allowed.
Do not upload events for executions of unknown binaries allowed in monitor mode.
If true, the client will not upload events for executions of unknown binaries allowed in monitor mode.
Content encoding used for sync requests.
Sets the Content-Encoding header for requests sent to the sync service.
Dictionary of additional headers to include in all requests made to the sync server. System-managed headers such as Content-Length, Host, and WWW-Authenticate will be ignored.
Dictionary of additional headers to include in all requests made to the sync server. System managed headers such as Content-Length, Host, WWW-Authenticate etc will be ignored.
Defines how event logs are stored. Allowed values: syslog, file, protobuf, json, null. Note: the protobuf and JSON formats are in BETA and subject to change.
Defines how event logs are stored. Allowed values: syslog, file, protobuf, json, null. Note: the protobuf and JSON formats are in BETA and subject to change.
Path to the event log file (used for file/json logging).
If EventLogType is set to file or json, EventLogPath will provide the path to save logs. If you change this value ensure you also update com.northpolesec.santa.newsyslog.conf with the new path.
Base directory for protobuf maildir-like logs.
If EventLogType is set to protobuf, SpoolDirectory will provide the base directory used to save files according to a maildir-like format.
Per-file size limit (KB) for protobuf spool files.

If EventLogType is set to protobuf, SpoolDirectoryFileSizeThresholdKB defines the per-file size limit for files stored in the spool directory. Events are buffered in memory until this threshold would be exceeded (or SpoolDirectoryEventMaxFlushTimeSec is exceeded).
Total size limit (MB) for protobuf spool directory.
If EventLogType is set to protobuf, SpoolDirectorySizeThresholdMB defines the total combined size limit of all files in the spool directory. Once the threshold is met, no more events will be saved.
Maximum time (seconds) events are buffered before flushing to disk.

If EventLogType is set to protobuf, SpoolDirectoryEventMaxFlushTimeSec defines the maximum amount of time events will stay buffered in memory before being flushed to disk, regardless of whether or not SpoolDirectoryFileSizeThresholdKB would be exceeded.
Appends the MachineID to the end of each log line if enabled.
If YES, this appends the MachineID to the end of each log line. Defaults to NO.
Filters entitlements from execution telemetry by prefix. Entitlements are only logged when EventLogType is set to protobuf or json.

Filters entitlements from execution telemetry based on prefix (for example: com.apple.private). Entitlements matching a prefix in this list will be omitted from the logged event. Entitlements are only logged when EventLogType is set to protobuf or json.
Filters entitlements from execution telemetry by Team ID. Entitlements are only logged when EventLogType is set to protobuf or json.

Filters entitlements from execution telemetry based on the process's TeamID. When a process's code signature has a TeamID matching an entry in this list, its entitlements will be omitted from the logged event. Entitlements are only logged when EventLogType is set to protobuf or json. Use the value platform to filter entitlements from platform binaries.
CEL expressions for filtering or redacting telemetry before upload. Only useful for Workshop customers.
A list of CEL expressions for filtering/redacting rows before upload. Only useful for Workshop customers.
Format to export metrics as, supported formats are "rawjson" for a single JSON blob and "monarchjson" for a format consumable by Google's Monarch tooling.
Format to export metrics as. Allowed values: rawjson: A single JSON blob containing all metrics. monarchjson: A format consumable by Google's internal Monarch tooling
URL describing where monitoring metrics should be exported.
URL describing where monitoring metrics should be exported.
Number of seconds to wait between exporting metrics. Defaults to 30.
Number of seconds to wait before a timeout occurs when exporting metrics. Defaults to 30.

A map of key value pairs to add to all metric root labels. If a previously set key (e.g. host_name) is set to "" then the key is removed from the metric root labels. Alternatively if a value is set for an existing key then the new value will override the old.

A map of key value pairs to add to all metric root labels. If a previously set key (e.g. host_name) is set to "" then the key is removed from the metric root labels. Alternatively if a value is set for an existing key then the new value will override the old.
Enable password authorization fallback for Standalone mode.
If true, Santa will fallback to password authorization for Standalone mode.
Ignore events generated by other EndpointSecurity clients.
If true, Santa will not process events that are generated by other EndpointSecurity clients that may be installed on the system
Send basic stats to North Pole Security
If true, Santa will periodically collect and send basic, non-identifying stats to the maintainers at North Pole Security to help better support Santa. See Stats documentation for complete details
North Pole Security customer ID for stats collection.
This key should only be set for organizations that have a contract with North Pole Security. See Stats documentation for complete details.
A list of Signing IDs to protect from `pid_suspend` calls.
A list of Signing IDs to protect from `pid_suspend` calls.
Array of allowed Santa commands. If unset, all commands are allowed; if set to an empty array, no commands are allowed.
Array of allowed Santa Commands. If the key AllowedSantaCommands is unset (nil), all commands are allowed. If AllowedSantaCommands is an empty array, none of the commands are allowed.
Allow signals delegated by launchd on behalf of Apple platform binaries targeting santad.
If true, signals delegated by 'launchd' on behalf of any Apple platform binary targeting 'santad' are allowed.
Path to a file access configuration plist.
Path to a file access configuration plist. This is ignored if FileAccessPolicy is also set. See File Access Authorization for configuration details.
How often (in seconds) to re-read the file access policy config.
Number of seconds between re-reading the file access policy config and policies/monitored paths being updated. The minimum value is 15 seconds.
Global override for the enforcement of all file access policy rules.

Defines a global override policy that applies to the enforcement of all FileAccessPolicy rules. AUDIT_ONLY: no access will be blocked, only logged. DISABLE: no access will be blocked or logged. none: enforce policy as defined in each rule.
Average number of file access logs per second. Set to 0 to disable rate limiting.

Sets the average logs per second that will be emitted by File Access Authorization rule violations. Setting to 0 will disable log rate limiting. Rate limiting only applies to logging. FAA rules that are not audit only will still block operations that violate the rule.
File Access Global Logs Per Second
Window size over which the log rate limit is applied. Set to 0 to disable rate limiting.

Sets the window size over which the FileAccessGlobalLogsPerSec setting is applied in order to allow for bursts of logs. Setting to 0 will disable log rate limiting. Rate limiting only applies to logging. FAA rules that are not audit only will still block operations that violate the rule.
Action applied to all removable media devices.
Action for all removable media. Allowed values: Allow, Block, Remount. If unset, falls back to deprecated BlockUSBMount and RemountUSBMode.
Mount flags applied when removable media is remounted.
Array of mount flag arguments when RemovableMediaAction is Remount. Allowed values: rdonly, noexec, nosuid, nobrowse, noowners, nodev, async, -j.
Action applied to encrypted removable media devices.
Action for encrypted removable media. Allowed values: Allow, Block, Remount. If unset, encrypted volumes use the baseline policy.
Mount flags applied when encrypted removable media is remounted.
Array of mount flag arguments for encrypted removable media when EncryptedRemovableMediaAction is Remount. Allowed values: rdonly, noexec, nosuid, nobrowse, noowners, nodev, async, -j.
Action applied to existing removable media when Santa starts.
Defines the action that should be taken on existing removable media mounts when Santa starts. Allowed values: Unmount, ForceUnmount, Remount, ForceRemount.
